issues_found: Critically narrow experimental scope: entire empirical foundation rests on ResNet-20 (270K params) on CIFAR-10/100 with AdamW. No ImageNet, no architecture diversity, no optimizer comparison. Project spec explicitly requires ImageNet. Three independent reviews (supervisor, final critic, Codex) identify this as the primary reason for rejection.

VGG-16-BN experiments not executed despite being listed as a P0 condition in the experiment decision. Batch normalization scale-invariance confound not resolved: ResNet-20+BN results may reflect BN rendering WD irrelevant, not AdamW absorbing WD modulation.

Only 3 seeds per configuration. Minimum detectable effect at 80% power is ~0.7% with σ≈0.3%, larger than observed differences. For a null-result paper, this is insufficient: cannot distinguish 'no effect' from 'effect too small to detect'. TOST at δ=±0.5% confirms only 1 of 12 comparisons as equivalent.

SGD CIFAR-100 data incomplete: only seed_42 for no_wd, missing all seeds for random_mask, and several other methods. The paper claims 49 total SGD experiments but this is unverifiable from the workspace. CIFAR-10 SGD is complete (7×3=21 runs).

Spatial and target-norm modulation axes not tested experimentally. Phi framework claims to cover four axes but experiments cover only temporal (SWD, cosine) and directional (CWD). AlphaDecay (spatial) and AdamWN (target-norm) are in Table 1 but not in the experiment suite.

Single hyperparameter setting λ=5×10⁻⁴. Codex review identifies critical confound: at this λ, WD is already a ~1% second-order perturbation of the adaptive gradient step, making the null result almost mechanically expected. Cannot distinguish 'AdamW absorbs WD modulation' from 'λ is too small for WD to matter'.

CWD falsification battery incomplete: only C2 (random_mask) executed. C1 (effective-lambda matched constant), C3 (inverted mask), C4 (soft cosine-weighted WD) not run. Cannot formally rule out that CWD's alignment mechanism matters through an alternative mechanism.

CSI component weights (0.4, 0.3, 0.3) are unjustified and the three components (CV of weight norm, log spectral condition number, CV of effective LR) are on different scales with no normalization specified. Appendix C referenced for sensitivity analysis but does not exist.

Naming inconsistencies not fully resolved: 'alignment-aware' vs 'directional,' 'cosine_schedule' vs 'cosine schedule' vs 'Cosine WD,' 'structural' vs 'spatial'. Critique reports these persist in the final manuscript.
Appendix B (diagnostic panels for all 42 runs) referenced in text but does not exist. Creates a broken reference that reviewers will notice.
issues_fixed: SGD data integrity error: original Table 5 had inflated p-values (SWD p=0.013 claimed, actual p=0.054; half_lambda p=0.028 claimed, actual p=0.062). Corrected in final draft to use actual values.
TOST equivalence testing added to Section 5.1 with pre-registered δ=±0.5% and δ=±1.0% margins.
Phi Invariance Conjecture scope narrowed from 'sufficiently overparameterized problem' to 'batch-normalized ResNet trained with AdamW on CIFAR-scale data, moderate λ≈5×10⁻⁴.'
Conclusion expanded from ~150 to ~400 words with structured subsections.
All 6 figures generated and visually verified (Figures 5 and 6 were previously placeholders).
AIS range corrected: using |ρ_S| absolute value for operational [0,1] interpretation, with correct statement that mathematical range is [-1,1].
BEM [0,1] boundedness claim qualified to 'within tested methods.'
SWD h(·) sensitivity function definition added to Table 1.
AdamWN phi direction clarified as 'target-norm projection' in Table 1.
